data.body: Plan-review PASS (advisory=11, signed; 5 criticals, all one point). Triage: the criticals correctly note _resolve_system resolves only the ~7 PASS-LEVEL prompts (finder/verifier/container/isf/completion/coach/novelty); the 41 per-CRITERION rubric bodies load via registry.get_prompt -> _criterion_block -> the RunRequest.instructions field, bypassing _resolve_system. But this is the RIGHT layer and is exactly what the ticket's 'What' section chose ('Add shared preamble at _resolve_system; do NOT invent a new layer'): the injection directive (G-12), forward-looking rule (FP-3c), and anti-thoroughness line (R-6) are pass-level reviewer STANCE, and every Pass-1/2 call sends the pass system prompt (with preamble) TOGETHER WITH the criterion rubric as instructions in one call — so the stance governs the whole call without duplicating it into 41 files. The 41 criterion files are hardened separately by the AC2 affirmative-framing sweep (text edits to their bodies). So AC1 (preamble) and AC2 (sweep) are complementary, not the same mechanism. Remediations: (1) AC1 test iterates the pass-level reviewer ids and asserts the preamble is present on each; (2) eval path corrected to src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-*.eval.yaml; (3) FP-3c is ADDED to the shared preamble (canonical) and left in F1 too (additive, no broken ref); (4) recall preserved by construction — the sweep edits reviewer PROMPTS, not the eval-spec datasets, so the recall cases are unchanged; baseline = current offline eval-spec case set (test_plan_review_evals green pre+post).
